label: RudderStack
description: Send and manage customer event data through RudderStack's open-source Customer Data Platform.

form.title: Connect RudderStack
form.subtitle: Paste your source Write Key and Data Plane URL to start ingesting events.

methods.label: Write Key + Data Plane URL
methods.description: Paste the Write Key from your RudderStack source and your Data Plane URL. The fastest path — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Write Key

Each RudderStack source has a unique Write Key that grants event ingestion access. You also need your Data Plane URL, which is where events are sent.

### Steps

1. Log in to [app.rudderstack.com](https://app.rudderstack.com).
2. In the left sidebar, go to **Sources** and click the source you want Anton to write to (or click **+ Add Source** to create a new one).
3. On the source detail page, find the **Write Key** field — it looks like a long alphanumeric string. Click **Copy**.
4. Paste the Write Key into the **Write Key** field below.
5. Now find your **Data Plane URL**:
   - For RudderStack Cloud, it appears on the same source page or under **Settings → Data Plane URL**. It typically looks like `https://<your-subdomain>.dataplane.rudderstack.com`.
   - For self-hosted deployments, use the URL of your own data plane server (e.g. `https://dp.yourcompany.com`).
6. Paste the Data Plane URL into the **Data Plane URL** field below.
7. Click **Submit**.

### Notes

- The Write Key is scoped to a single source — create a dedicated source (type: **HTTP API**) for Anton to keep traffic isolated.
- If you regenerate the Write Key in RudderStack, come back here and update it.
- Self-hosted data planes must be reachable from the machine running Anton.

fields.name: write_key
fields.label: Write Key
fields.description: Found on your source's detail page in the RudderStack dashboard. Treat it like a password.

fields.name: data_plane_url
fields.label: Data Plane URL
fields.description: The endpoint where events are sent. For RudderStack Cloud, copy it from the source page. For self-hosted, use your own data plane URL.

methods.label: Personal Access Token (Management API)
methods.description: Use a RudderStack Personal Access Token to call the management API — for reading source/destination configs, not just ingesting events.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Personal Access Token

RudderStack's management API lets you read and configure sources, destinations, and transformations. It uses Personal Access Tokens for authentication.

### Steps

1. Log in to [app.rudderstack.com](https://app.rudderstack.com).
2. Click your **profile icon** in the bottom-left corner and select **Profile**.
3. Scroll down to the **Personal Access Tokens** section.
4. Click **Generate new token**, give it a name like `Anton`, and select a role (**Read-Only** is sufficient for reading configs; **Read-Write** is needed for mutations).
5. Copy the token — it is shown only once.
6. Paste it into the **Personal Access Token** field below.
7. Optionally provide your **Data Plane URL** if you also want to send events.

### Notes

- Tokens are shown only once at creation. If you lose it, delete it and generate a new one.
- Use **Read-Only** role for lowest-privilege access when Anton only needs to inspect config.
- The management API base URL is `https://api.rudderstack.com` for RudderStack Cloud.

fields.name: personal_access_token
fields.label: Personal Access Token
fields.description: Generated from your RudderStack profile page. Shown only once — store it safely.

fields.name: data_plane_url
fields.label: Data Plane URL
fields.description: Optional. Provide if Anton should also send events in addition to managing configs.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Region
fields.description: RudderStack Cloud region your workspace is hosted in.
